close_reason: Self-cancelled: description concluded 'keep as is, idiomatic and clear'. Bikeshedding per /review-rusty-crate rules.
description:
lib.rs:153-160:
```rust
let addr = parsed
    .param("addr")
    .map(|s| s.to_string())
    .or_else(|| std::env::var("VAULT_ADDR").ok())
    .unwrap_or_else(|| "http://127.0.0.1:8200".to_string());
let addr = addr.trim_end_matches('/').to_string();
```

The chain is idiomatic Rust. The minor smell is the .to_string() / unwrap_or_else / trim_end_matches / .to_string() round-trip — three String allocations to settle on the final value. A reviewer might be tempted to simplify this. The current form is more readable than alternatives that chase allocations.

The trim_end_matches('/') line is good and well-commented. The whole block could be a small helper fn resolve_addr() if a peer crate ever reuses the pattern — file the cross-crate consideration on the workspace epic, not here.

P3/opinion — keep as is, idiomatic and clear. Filing only to record the audit trail.
title: addr default chain reads VAULT_ADDR after URI param — consider documenting precedence in struct field
